name: Fix Renderer Output

tasks.title: Reproduce and diagnose raw NDJSON output
tasks.description: Reproduce and diagnose raw NDJSON output
tasks.body:
Run wolfcastle log --thoughts and wolfcastle log --interleaved against an existing session and confirm both dump raw NDJSON instead of formatted text. Then trace the pipeline to find where formatting is lost. Key files to investigate:

- cmd/daemon/follow.go: runLog() dispatches to renderers at lines 136-163. Verify the correct renderer is instantiated and Render() is called.
- internal/logrender/reader.go: ReplayReader.Records() and FollowReader.Records() parse NDJSON and emit Record structs. Verify records are being parsed (not passed as raw strings).
- internal/logrender/thoughts.go: ThoughtsRenderer.Render() filters for type==assistant and prints r.Text. Verify this path executes.
- internal/logrender/interleaved.go: InterleavedRenderer.Render() handles stage_start, stage_complete, assistant, etc. Verify this path executes.

Possible failure modes: (a) renderer is constructed but never receives records from the channel, (b) records arrive but fail the type checks, (c) a fallback path dumps raw JSON, (d) the wrong output mode is selected, (e) the reader silently fails to parse and some other codepath writes stdout.

tasks.title: Fix thoughts renderer to match spec
tasks.description: Fix thoughts renderer to match spec
tasks.body:
After diagnosing the root cause, fix the thoughts renderer so that wolfcastle log --thoughts produces spec-compliant output: plain text extracted from assistant-type NDJSON records, one thought per line, no stage headers, no timestamps, no JSON. The spec example:

I'll start by creating the site specification document...
Reading the project requirements from the inbox item...
The inbox item asks for a donut stand website, so I'll need...

Both replay mode (historical session) and follow mode (tailing live daemon) must work. Update thoughts_test.go if the fix requires test changes.

tasks.title: Fix interleaved renderer to match spec
tasks.description: Fix interleaved renderer to match spec
tasks.body:
After diagnosing the root cause, fix the interleaved renderer so that wolfcastle log --interleaved produces spec-compliant output: stage headers with wall-clock timestamps and glyphs, agent text indented below. The spec example:

18:01:34 ▶ [execute] donut-stand-website/site-specification/task-0001
18:01:35     I'll start by creating the site specification document...
18:01:36     Reading the project requirements from the inbox item...
18:02:56 ✓ [execute] donut-stand-website/site-specification/task-0001 (1m22s)

Key formatting rules:
- Wall-clock timestamps in HH:MM:SS local time
- ▶ for stage starts, ✓ for success, ✗ for failure
- Agent thoughts indented 5 spaces after timestamp
- audit_report_written paths indented below audit completion line
- No sequence numbers, no log levels, no terminal markers

Both replay and follow modes must work. Update interleaved_test.go if the fix requires test changes.

tasks.title: Verify renderer fixes with tests
tasks.body:
Run the full test suite for the logrender package and the parity tests in cmd/execute_test.go. All existing tests must pass. If the fix changed behavior that existing tests assert against, those tests should have been updated in the previous tasks. Run:

go test ./internal/logrender/... -v
go test ./cmd/... -v -run TestRenderParity

Confirm that thoughts output contains only plain text (no JSON), and interleaved output contains timestamped stage headers with glyphed agent text (no raw JSON lines).

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Fix Renderer Output is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Confirm thoughts output contains zero JSON braces or colons from record structure, only plain text lines
Confirm interleaved output has HH:MM:SS timestamps, glyph characters, and indented agent text on every relevant line

breadcrumbs.text: Fixed thoughts and interleaved renderers: both now call invoke.FormatAssistantText to extract human-readable content from Claude API JSON envelopes stored in Record.Text. Added regression tests with real envelope formats.

task-0001.objective: Diagnose and fix raw NDJSON output from wolfcastle log --thoughts and --interleaved
task-0001.what_happened: Traced the pipeline from cmd/daemon/follow.go through logrender readers to renderers. The renderers correctly consumed Record structs and checked r.Type == assistant, but r.Text contained the raw Claude API JSON envelope (escaped as a string) rather than plain text. The daemon's assistantLogWriter captures Claude Code's stdout verbatim, which emits NDJSON with nested JSON envelopes. The existing invoke.FormatAssistantText function already knew how to parse these envelopes. Applied it in both ThoughtsRenderer and InterleavedRenderer.
went_well: invoke.FormatAssistantText already existed with full test coverage, so the fix was surgical: two import additions and two call sites
All existing tests continued passing because FormatAssistantText passes plain text through unchanged
improvements: The assistantLogWriter could extract text at write-time to avoid the double-encoding entirely, but that would change the log format contract
action_items: Consider whether the log format should store pre-extracted text vs raw envelopes as a deliberate design decision (ADR-worthy)
